D1 Optics L2 Understand Reference

EM Wave Propagation — Right-Hand Rule

rotate E and B around the unit triad and watch the direction of travel rotate with them

The direction of propagation k of any electromagnetic wave is the cross product of its electric and magnetic field vectors — chosen by the right-hand rule.

Slide the rotation. E (crimson) and B (gold) rotate together in the yz-plane; the propagation vector k stays orthogonal to both — that's the right-hand rule in action.
